docs.title: Home
docs.text:
Qubic Capital – Whitepaper

Whitepaper & Structural Documentation

This document provides a clear and structured overview of Qubic Capital, its governance framework, capital formation model, tokenomics, revenue distribution logic, and long-term strategic positioning within the Qubic ecosystem.

This documentation is intended to provide transparency, structural clarity, and long-term alignment for all participants and stakeholders.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text:
Qubic Capital enables everyone, regardless of the size of their portfolio, to own shares in smart contracts while maintaining diversification. Through this project, members have the opportunity to participate in Qubic’s IPO, an event typically reserved for the wealthiest investors, and to become co-owners of an extensive portfolio, the largest available, of shares representing the entire Qubic ecosystem.

Investment decisions are subject to a majority vote by eligible members before implementation. All participants can become eligible members with voting power, with voting weight determined by their holdings and participation in governance.

Governance, capital allocation, fundraising, and revenue distribution are managed automatically and in a decentralized manner through QVAULT, the smart contract that administers all operational aspects of Qubic Capital. This approach ensures that all participants who engage in governance have a say in the direction of the project.

docs.title: Smart Contracts in Qubic
docs.text:
Smart Contracts are blockchain-based, self-acting protocols that activate when specified code criteria are met. They represent one of the core applications of blockchain technology. Qubic introduces its own implementation of Smart Contracts, combining efficiency and security within its protocol.

The shares associated with a Smart Contract undergo an IPO using a Dutch auction model. Only 676 shares are available per smart contract. This structure creates:

  • Fixed supply
  • Structural scarcity
  • Competitive allocation
  • Increased economic value concentration

For Qubic’s very first IPO, the Smart Contract QX was launched, and each of the 676 shares was priced at 15b, a significant amount for a small investor.

docs.title: Ownership Concentration in the Qubic Ecosystem
docs.text:
In many emerging ecosystems, ownership of scarce, revenue-generating assets tends to become concentrated among a limited number of participants.

Within the Qubic ecosystem, data observed since the creation of Qubic Capital in 2024 show a significant concentration of dividend-generating Smart Contract shares among a relatively small group of holders.

This concentration reflects the structural reality that access to Smart Contract IPOs requires substantial capital and rapid execution. Each Smart Contract offers only 676 shares, and IPO participation operates under competitive auction dynamics. As a result, prices can reach levels that are not economically accessible to many individual participants.

The combination of fixed scarcity, competitive allocation mechanisms, and limited supply creates a structural barrier to entry. Qubic Capital provides a coordinated mechanism that allows broader participation in the ownership of dividend-generating Smart Contracts, thereby reducing these access constraints at the individual level.

docs.title: The Qubic Capital Solution
docs.text:
Smaller investors pool their resources to invest collectively, creating an investment structure that enables access to opportunities that might be out of reach individually. By joining forces, these investors diversify their investments and share potential returns.

Qubic Capital enables participants to take part in Initial Public Offerings, providing access to early-stage Smart Contract shares. It also enables participation in the acquisition of high-priced Smart Contract shares in the secondary market, offering broader exposure beyond primary issuances. This structure allows individual investors to benefit from both early-stage and secondary market opportunities that might otherwise be reserved for larger players.

In addition, Qubic Capital contributes to the development of DeFi (decentralized finance) within the Qubic ecosystem. Qbond is the first solution powered by Qubic Capital, designed to support the broader ecosystem while generating additional revenue streams. These revenues contribute to the overall performance of Qubic Capital and provide extra value to $QCAP holders.

docs.title: Strategic Vision
docs.text:
Qubic Capital positions itself as the benchmark investment vehicle within the Qubic ecosystem, serving as the primary gateway to diversified, ecosystem-wide exposure. By allocating capital across multiple revenue-generating Smart Contracts and strategic initiatives, it operates as an ETF-like structure that provides systemic participation in the growth of the ecosystem.

Unlike single-asset exposure strategies, Qubic Capital offers diversified systemic exposure through a single token.

Qubic Capital already represents a significant share of dividend-generating Smart Contract ownership within the ecosystem, reinforcing its benchmark status. Its ambition is to continue expanding this participation over time, strengthening portfolio depth and broadening exposure across the most relevant initiatives within Qubic.

By holding $QCAP, participants gain access to revenue generated by the underlying portfolio, including Smart Contract dividends and DeFi products powered by Qubic Capital. In addition to these recurring revenues, holders may also benefit from the appreciation of the token price as the portfolio expands and ecosystem adoption grows. The token reflects the performance of the portfolio, aligning holders with long-term ecosystem growth.

docs.title: On-Chain Governance
docs.text:
Qubic Capital operates under a decentralized governance framework executed through QVAULT.

All strategic and operational decisions are implemented through predefined proposal types within QVAULT, including but not limited to:

  • Quorum percentage
  • Revenue allocation
  • Fundraising
  • IPO participation
  • Marketplace proposals

Voting rights are exercised through locked $QCAP in QVAULT.

Each proposal follows predefined validation rules enforced by QVAULT, including quorum and majority requirements. Execution occurs automatically once the approval conditions are met.

docs.title: Representatives
docs.text:
In addition to the on-chain governance layer, Qubic Capital designates seven representatives elected by the community. These representatives serve as administrators of the official Discord server and act as public-facing coordinators of Qubic Capital within the broader Qubic ecosystem.

Their role is not to override governance decisions, but to:

  • Facilitate communication
  • Represent Qubic Capital externally
  • Coordinate discussions
  • Ensure transparency
  • Safeguard the collective interests of all $QCAP holders

The representatives operate within the governance framework and remain accountable to the holder base.

docs.title: Supply
docs.text:
The total maximum supply of $QCAP equals 21,000,000 tokens, of which 3,000,000 tokens form part of the reserve.

The emission schedule adjusts according to market conditions, the broader cryptocurrency cycle, and the development of Qubic. Decisions on issuance are determined by holders through governance proposals and votes executed in QVAULT.

New $QCAP tokens are released exclusively through governance-approved fundraising rounds. There are no automatic emissions or uncontrolled supply increases.

Each new issuance is tied to a defined capital allocation plan, typically participation in upcoming IPOs or the acquisition of additional revenue-generating assets. The capital raised is deployed directly into portfolio expansion.

This mechanism prevents economic dilution. When new tokens are issued, the increase in supply is matched by a corresponding increase in underlying portfolio assets. Token issuance therefore represents capitalization of new assets rather than redistribution of existing value, maintaining alignment between circulating supply and intrinsic portfolio value per token.

docs.title: Reserve
docs.text: The reserved allocation serves strategic purposes such as ecosystem alignment, incentives, partnerships, or long-term development. The specific use of the reserve is determined by holders through proposals and votes in QVAULT.

docs.title: Revenue Distribution
docs.text:
Revenue distribution is structured as follows:

  • 3% goes to QVAULT smart contract shareholders, immutable
  • The remaining 97% is allocated among distribution to QCAP holders, the reinvestment fund, and QCAP burn, not available before 1 January 2029

Revenue generated from the assets held by Qubic Capital is redistributed directly in $QUBIC. Redistribution occurs at the end of each epoch, on Wednesday at 12:00 UTC, and is made to holders existing at the end of the epoch in proportion to their eligible balance.

docs.title: Portfolio Valuation Framework
docs.text:
Qubic Capital maintains a structured and transparent valuation framework for its underlying portfolio.

Portfolio valuation follows these principles:

  • Asset-level valuation based on observable market prices on QX
  • Use of averaged recent trades to reduce short-term volatility
  • Inclusion of all held Smart Contract shares, DeFi positions, and cash balances
  • Periodic disclosure of total portfolio value and circulating supply

The intrinsic value per token is derived by dividing the net portfolio value attributable to $QCAP holders by the circulating supply.

This framework ensures transparency, consistency, and traceability in how the value of $QCAP is assessed over time.

docs.title: How to Buy $QCAP
docs.text: $QCAP is acquired through two complementary markets.

docs.title: Primary Market
docs.text:
In the Primary Market, new $QCAP tokens are issued directly by Qubic Capital through governance-approved fundraising rounds executed in QVAULT.

Each round specifies:

  • The amount of $QCAP to be issued
  • The price per token

These parameters are decided collectively by eligible voters.

Participation in the Primary Market directly strengthens Qubic Capital, as the capital raised is allocated to Smart Contract acquisitions, ecosystem initiatives, and revenue-generating opportunities. This expands the portfolio and reinforces long-term ecosystem growth.

Primary rounds are conducted at a fixed price defined in the proposal, ensuring equal conditions for all participants.

docs.title: Secondary Market
docs.text:
$QCAP is also acquired through peer-to-peer trading or QX (Qubic Exchange).

The Secondary Market provides liquidity and flexibility, allowing participants to adjust their positions at any time. Prices are determined by supply and demand.

While the Secondary Market enhances tradability and visibility, it does not directly fund Qubic Capital. In contrast, the Primary Market injects new capital into the ecosystem and expands the underlying portfolio base.

Both markets complement each other:

  • Primary Market drives capital formation and portfolio expansion
  • Secondary Market provides liquidity and price discovery

docs.title: Long-Term Alignment
docs.text:
Qubic Capital is structured to align governance, capital formation, and revenue generation under a single coordinated framework.

Holders participate in decision-making through QVAULT, capital raised through approved fundraising expands the portfolio, and revenues generated by underlying assets flow back to holders according to predefined distribution rules. This creates structural alignment between supply growth and portfolio expansion.

As the Qubic ecosystem evolves, Qubic Capital aims to remain a benchmark vehicle for diversified exposure, capital coordination, and long-term participation in ecosystem growth.

The model is designed for sustainability rather than short-term speculation, reinforcing alignment between holders and portfolio development over time.
